entries.definition:
كتاب الجيم

جعف - جعل

(جعل) الجيم والعين واللام كلمات غير مُنْقاسة، لا يشبه بعضُها بعضاً. فالجَعْلُ: النَّخْلُ يفوت اليدَ، والواحدةُ جَعْلة. وهو قوله:

* أو يستَوي جَثِيُثها وجَعْلُها *

والجَعْوَل: ولد النعام. والجِعَال: الخِرْقة التي تُنْزَلُ بها القِدْر عن الأثافي. والجُعْل والجَُِعالة والجَعيلة: ما يُجعل للإنسان على الأمر يَفعلُه. وجعَلْتُ الشيءَ صنعتُه. قال الخليل: إلاَّ أنَّ جَعلَ أعمُّ، تقول جَعَل يقول، ولا تقول صَنعَ يقول.

j–ʿ–l reward/payment set for a task to be carried out, bribe; to put, to place; to make, to create, to constitute; to attribute; scarab, seedling. Of this root, four forms occur 245 times in the Qurʾan: جَعَلَ jaʿala 238 times; جُعِلَ juʿila once; جَاعِل jāʿil four times and جَاعِلُون jāʿilūn twice.
165
entries.definition_english:
j–ʿ–l reward/payment set for a task to be carried out, bribe; to put, to place; to make, to create, to constitute; to attribute; scarab, seedling. Of this root, four forms occur 245 times in the Qurʾan: جَعَلَ jaʿala 238 times; جُعِلَ juʿila once; جَاعِل jāʿil four times and جَاعِلُون jāʿilūn twice.
165

entries.definition:
جَعَلَ jaʿala a I [v. trans.] 1 to place (12:70) فَلَمَّا جَهَّزَهُمْ بِجَهَازِهِمْ جَعَلَ السِّقَايَةَ فِي رَحْلِ أَخِيهِ and when he provided them with their provisions, he placed the drinking-cup in the pack of his brother 2 to instil (57:27) وَجَعَلْنَا فِي قُلُوبِ الَّذِينَ اتَّبَعُوهُ رَأْفَةً وَرَحْمَةً and We instilled into the hearts of those who followed him compassion and mercy 3 to construct, raise, put up (17:45) وَإِذَا قَرَأْتَ الْقُرْآنَ جَعَلْنَا بَيْنَكَ وَبَيْنَ الَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْآخِرَةِ حِجَابًا مَسْتُورًا when you recite the Qurʾan, We place between you and those who do not believe in the life to come a hidden barrier 4 to create, to make, to bring into being (6:1) الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَجَعَلَ الظُّلُمَاتِ وَالنُّورَ praise belongs to God who created the heavens and the earth and made darkness and light 5 to ordain, to bring about (60:7) عَسَى اللَّهُ أَنْ يَجْعَلَ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَ الَّذِينَ عَادَيْتُمْ مِنْهُمْ مَوَدَّةً God may well bring about affection between you and those of them you face as an enemy 6 to provide with, to appoint for (16:80) وَاللَّهُ جَعَلَ لَكُمْ مِنْ بُيُوتِكُمْ سَكَنًا وَجَعَلَ لَكُمْ مِنْ جُلُودِ الْأَنْعَامِ بُيُوتًا تَسْتَخِفُّونَهَا يَوْمَ ظَعْنِكُمْ وَيَوْمَ إِقَامَتِكُمْ and He has appointed for you, of the hide of cattle, dwellings that you find light on the day you travel and on the day you settle down 7 to fix as a tribute or reward (18:94) فَهَلْ نَجْعَلُ لَكَ خَرْجًا عَلَىٰ أَنْ تَجْعَلَ بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَهُمْ سَدًّا so may we fix a tribute for you on condition that you set up a barrier between us and them? 8 to attribute something to, to assign to (16:57) وَيَجْعَلُونَ لِلَّهِ الْبَنَاتِ سُبْحَانَهُ they assign daughters to God–may He be exalted! 9 [with prep. لِـ] to prepare, to facilitate, to find (65:2) وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مَخْرَجًا whosoever is mindful of God, God will find a way [out of difficulties] for him 10 [with prep. عَلَى] to put in charge (12:55) قَالَ اجْعَلْنِي عَلَىٰ خَزَائِنِ الْأَرْضِ [Joseph] said, ‘Put me in charge of the nation’s storehouses’ II [doubly trans.] 1 to appoint someone/something as, to make somebody/something into (6:91) قُلْ مَنْ أَنْزَلَ الْكِتَابَ الَّذِي جَاءَ بِهِ مُوسَىٰ نُورًا وَهُدًى لِلنَّاسِ تَجْعَلُونَهُ قَرَاطِيسَ تُبْدُونَهَا وَتُخْفُونَ كَثِيرًا say, ‘Who was it who sent down the Scripture, which Moses brought as a light and a guide to people, which you made into separate sheets, showing [some of] them but hiding many?’ 2 to appoint somebody/something as (23:50) وَجَعَلْنَا ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ وَأُمَّهُ آيَةً and We appointed the son of Mary and his mother as an exemplar 3 to constitute something as, to make something be (25:45) أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ كَيْفَ مَدَّ الظِّلَّ وَلَوْ شَاءَ لَجَعَلَهُ سَاكِنًا have you not considered the way of your Lord, how He lengthens the shadow?; had He willed, He could have created it still
166

جَعَلَ jaʿala a I [v. trans.] 1 to place (12:70) فَلَمَّا جَهَّزَهُمْ بِجَهَازِهِمْ جَعَلَ السِّقَايَةَ فِي رَحْلِ أَخِيهِ and when he provided them with their provisions, he placed the drinking-cup in the pack of his brother 2 to instil (57:27) وَجَعَلْنَا فِي قُلُوبِ الَّذِينَ اتَّبَعُوهُ رَأْفَةً وَرَحْمَةً and We instilled into the hearts of those who followed him compassion and mercy 3 to construct, raise, put up (17:45) وَإِذَا قَرَأْتَ الْقُرْآنَ جَعَلْنَا بَيْنَكَ وَبَيْنَ الَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْآخِرَةِ حِجَابًا مَسْتُورًا when you recite the Qurʾan, We place between you and those who do not believe in the life to come a hidden barrier 4 to create, to make, to bring into being (6:1) الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَجَعَلَ الظُّلُمَاتِ وَالنُّورَ praise belongs to God who created the heavens and the earth and made darkness and light 5 to ordain, to bring about (60:7) عَسَى اللَّهُ أَنْ يَجْعَلَ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَ الَّذِينَ عَادَيْتُمْ مِنْهُمْ مَوَدَّةً God may well bring about affection between you and those of them you face as an enemy 6 to provide with, to appoint for (16:80) وَاللَّهُ جَعَلَ لَكُمْ مِنْ بُيُوتِكُمْ سَكَنًا وَجَعَلَ لَكُمْ مِنْ جُلُودِ الْأَنْعَامِ بُيُوتًا تَسْتَخِفُّونَهَا يَوْمَ ظَعْنِكُمْ وَيَوْمَ إِقَامَتِكُمْ and He has appointed for you, of the hide of cattle, dwellings that you find light on the day you travel and on the day you settle down 7 to fix as a tribute or reward (18:94) فَهَلْ نَجْعَلُ لَكَ خَرْجًا عَلَىٰ أَنْ تَجْعَلَ بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَهُمْ سَدًّا so may we fix a tribute for you on condition that you set up a barrier between us and them? 8 to attribute something to, to assign to (16:57) وَيَجْعَلُونَ لِلَّهِ الْبَنَاتِ سُبْحَانَهُ they assign daughters to God–may He be exalted! 9 [with prep. لِـ] to prepare, to facilitate, to find (65:2) وَمَنْ يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجْعَلْ لَهُ مَخْرَجًا whosoever is mindful of God, God will find a way [out of difficulties] for him 10 [with prep. عَلَى] to put in charge (12:55) قَالَ اجْعَلْنِي عَلَىٰ خَزَائِنِ الْأَرْضِ [Joseph] said, ‘Put me in charge of the nation’s storehouses’ II [doubly trans.] 1 to appoint someone/something as, to make somebody/something into (6:91) قُلْ مَنْ أَنْزَلَ الْكِتَابَ الَّذِي جَاءَ بِهِ مُوسَىٰ نُورًا وَهُدًى لِلنَّاسِ تَجْعَلُونَهُ قَرَاطِيسَ تُبْدُونَهَا وَتُخْفُونَ كَثِيرًا say, ‘Who was it who sent down the Scripture, which Moses brought as a light and a guide to people, which you made into separate sheets, showing [some of] them but hiding many?’ 2 to appoint somebody/something as (23:50) وَجَعَلْنَا ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ وَأُمَّهُ آيَةً and We appointed the son of Mary and his mother as an exemplar 3 to constitute something as, to make something be (25:45) أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ كَيْفَ مَدَّ الظِّلَّ وَلَوْ شَاءَ لَجَعَلَهُ سَاكِنًا have you not considered the way of your Lord, how He lengthens the shadow?; had He willed, He could have created it still
